This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

**Dear reviewer**
This pull request is ready for review.

I will implement them.
@registerrier The default value was not motivated. As you said it should depend on the FoV radius and bin size (reducing to the number of pixels).

A quick estimate is : 

Deviation at R for rotation of d_theta: 2 pi R * d_theta / 360 deg
size of pixel for a FOV of radius R and n_pix pixels per dimension: 2R/n_pix

If we want the deviation to be less than the size of a pixel: 
2 pi R * d_theta / 360 deg < 2R/n_pix
d_theta < 720 deg / (2 pi npix)
taking n_pix ~ 100 and using 2 pi ~ 7.2  we have
d_theta <~ 1 deg

So using this logic, 0.5 deg is not bad. But I would be OK with changing this value.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/issues/comments/2391233389/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/pulls/comments/1786108388","pull_request_review_id":2345425888,"id":1786108388,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAJm2KM5qddnk","diff_hunk":"@@ -98,6 +98,38 @@ def _get_fov_coords(pointing, irf, geom, use_region_center=True, obstime=None):
     return coords
 
 
+def time_limit_rotation(maximum_rotation, pointing_altaz):
+    """
+    Compute the maximum time such that the FoV associated to a fixed RaDec position rotates by less than
+    'maximum_rotation' in AltAz frame. It assumes that the rotation rate at the provided pointing is a good estimate
+    of the rotation rate over the full output duration (first order approximation).
+
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    maximum_rotation : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Maximum rotation angle.
+    pointing_altaz : `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+        Pointing direction.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    duration : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Time associated with the requested rotation.
+    """
+    earth_rotation_deg_per_second = 0.004167
+    denom = (
+        earth_rotation_deg_per_second
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.location.lat.rad)
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.az.rad)
+    )
+    if denom == 0:
+        return 3600 * u.s","path":"gammapy/makers/utils.py","commit_id":"9e45af09cd33ea3d8926fd9c3c271bfa07b3d126","original_commit_id":"9e45af09cd33ea3d8926fd9c3c271bfa07b3d126","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"I wanted a way to handle the case when the denominator is 0.
Which occurs when the telescope is at one of the poles (which is constant so we could have an infinite time step),
or when the pointing az is +-90 deg. In the latter case, this is temporary so the interval needs to be limited. 

Thinking a bit more about this, one solution would be to use the derivative of this function.
The 1 hour default is okay as long as we are not very close to Zenith. 
I would be happy to have a better solution to handle this edge alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

     return coords
 
 
+def time_limit_rotation(maximum_rotation, pointing_altaz):
+    """
+    Compute the maximum time such that the FoV associated to a fixed RaDec position rotates by less than
+    'maximum_rotation' in AltAz frame. It assumes that the rotation rate at the provided pointing is a good estimate
+    of the rotation rate over the full output duration (first order approximation).
+
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    maximum_rotation : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Maximum rotation angle.
+    pointing_altaz : `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+        Pointing direction.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    duration : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Time associated with the requested rotation.
+    """
+    earth_rotation_deg_per_second = 0.004167
+    denom = (
+        earth_rotation_deg_per_second
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.location.lat.rad)
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.az.rad)
+    )
+    if denom == 0:
+        return 3600 * u.s
+    return (

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

**Dear reviewer**
This pull request is ready for review.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

{"action":"created","comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/gammapy/gammapy/pulls/comments/1789805996","pull_request_review_id":2351327457,"id":1789805996,"node_id":"PRRC_kwDOAJm2KM5qrkWs","diff_hunk":"@@ -98,6 +98,38 @@ def _get_fov_coords(pointing, irf, geom, use_region_center=True, obstime=None):
     return coords
 
 
+def time_limit_rotation(maximum_rotation, pointing_altaz):
+    """
+    Compute the maximum time such that the FoV associated to a fixed RaDec position rotates by less than
+    'maximum_rotation' in AltAz frame. It assumes that the rotation rate at the provided pointing is a good estimate
+    of the rotation rate over the full output duration (first order approximation).
+
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    maximum_rotation : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Maximum rotation angle.
+    pointing_altaz : `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+        Pointing direction.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    duration : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Time associated with the requested rotation.
+    """
+    earth_rotation_deg_per_second = 0.004167
+    denom = (
+        earth_rotation_deg_per_second
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.location.lat.rad)
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.az.rad)
+    )
+    if denom == 0:

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

     return coords
 
 
+def time_limit_rotation(maximum_rotation, pointing_altaz):
+    """
+    Compute the maximum time such that the FoV associated to a fixed RaDec position rotates by less than
+    'maximum_rotation' in AltAz frame. It assumes that the rotation rate at the provided pointing is a good estimate
+    of the rotation rate over the full output duration (first order approximation).
+
+
+    Parameters
+    ----------
+    maximum_rotation : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Maximum rotation angle.
+    pointing_altaz : `~astropy.coordinates.SkyCoord`
+        Pointing direction.
+
+    Returns
+    -------
+    duration : `~astropy.units.Quantity`
+        Time associated with the requested rotation.
+    """
+    earth_rotation_deg_per_second = 0.004167
+    denom = (
+        earth_rotation_deg_per_second
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.location.lat.rad)
+        * np.cos(pointing_altaz.az.rad)
+    )
+    if denom == 0:

alignment","user":{"login":"gabemery","id":35919817,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjM1OTE5ODE3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/35919817?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery","html_url":"https://github.com/gabemery","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/gabemery/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"**Description**

This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.

bscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adonath/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adonath/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adonath/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adonath/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/adonath/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":125,"closed_issues":1,"state":"open","created_at":"2022-09-02T14:05:54Z","updated_at":"2024-09-20T09:57:09Z","due_on":"2025-03-31T07:00:00Z","closed_at":null},"comments":3,"created_at":"2023-10-24T18:36:06Z","updated_at":"2024-09-24T09:50:22Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"In the code of the `make_map_background_irf()` function, two TODOs were mentioned by @adonath and still need to be fixed. https://github.com/gammapy/gammapy/blob/c5c1a5b66f9f8574bf10bacb7e3d4623995b264a/gammapy/makers/utils.py#L154 

1. Create equal time intervals between TSTART and TSTOP and sum up the background IRF for each interval. This is instead of multiplying by the total ontime. This then handles the rotation of the FoV.
2. Use the pointing table (does not currently exist in CTA files) to obtain the RA DEC and time for each interval. This aims at improving the `make_map_background_irf` method when the background IRF is 3d with an AltAz alignment.
This covers the first point of issue #4860 (and the associated TODO in the code) but with a different logic than the one mentioned there.

Current limitation : the `make_map_background_irf` used to evaluate the background counts in RaDec applies a single frame transformation when using a background IRF defined in AltAz alignment. This means that if the field of view rotates during the observation, the background will be miss estimated if it is not rotation symmetric.

Proposed solution : Re-evaluate the background IRF on smaller time intervals. The original proposal was to use fixed time intervals. I have instead implemented a solution where the time intervals are created based on a maximum error on the FOV rotation.

Logic of the criteria : The rotation rate of the Radec frame in AltAz is a function of the observatory latitude, and pointing in altaz. Using this first order derivative, a given limit on rotation can be converted in a maximum observation time. It should be valid as long as the rotation rate doesn't change too fast during the output time window.
